_about.purpose: Phase 0 refactor safety net. Machine-checkable inventory of normative rules, their canonical owner doc, where they may be linked, and where they must NOT be restated. Drives tests/test_rule_inventory.py. Adding/moving doc prose in later phases must keep this inventory green.
_about.format_note: JSON (not YAML) so the test suite stays standard-library-only, matching the skill's no-dependencies policy.

status_values.canonical: Rule already lives in exactly one owner; anti-duplication is enforced now.
status_values.duplicated-pending: Rule is currently restated across several docs. Phase 1-4 will collapse it; until then the test only RECORDS the duplication, it does not fail on it. Flip to 'canonical' (and fill forbidden_locations) in the commit that deduplicates it.

field_reference.assertions: lowercased substrings that MUST appear in canonical_owner
field_reference.pointer_locations: docs that must mention the owner's filename (a link, not a restatement)
field_reference.forbidden_locations: [{file, signatures[]}] substrings that must NOT appear outside the owner (anti-duplication)
field_reference.cross_links: [{file, must_contain[]}] reference paths that must appear in file
field_reference.ordering: [{file, sequence[]}] lowercased substrings that must appear in this order within file
field_reference.dup_signature: for duplicated-pending: a lowercased substring whose spread across docs is the duplication being tracked
field_reference.guarded_by: existing test method in test_concept_analysis_docs.py, or 'none' for coverage added by Phase 0

rules.rule: workflow.md owns the 13-step build sequence and the MeSH/free-text/proximity mental model; primary docs link, never restate.
assertions: formal concept analysis and concept gate
(mesh layer or title/abstract layer or proximity/wildcard layer)
mesh does not replace free text
free text does not replace mesh
proximity and wildcards do not replace either

signatures: (mesh layer or title/abstract layer or proximity/wildcard layer)
mesh does not replace free text
(mesh layer or title/abstract layer or proximity/wildcard layer)
mesh does not replace free text
(mesh layer or title/abstract layer or proximity/wildcard layer)
mesh does not replace free text

rules.rule: concept-analysis-and-gating.md owns the role taxonomy, the concept-analysis ledger fields, and the AND-block admission test.
SKILL.md owns the two-level stage-reporting contract and the six full-banner fields.
goal-tracking.md owns the /goal state machine; SKILL.md only links to it and must not restate the states.
seed-pmid-validation.md owns pre-gate seed triage mechanics; other docs reference the policy.
assertions: retain it as a special validation seed
do not use malformed, missing, excluded, or unresolved seed records as term evidence

rules.rule: mesh-and-pubmed-tools.md owns the record-content rule: fetch/mine/sample require --output, no --summary, inspect saved JSON.
mesh-and-pubmed-tools.md documents the manifest_tool.py helper and run_manifest.json provenance ledger.
wildcard-and-truncation.md owns the 600-variant cap sensitivity hazard and the truncation/proximity mutual exclusion.
assertions: truncation and proximity are mutually exclusive

rules.rule: validated-methodological-filters-and-hedges.md owns the validated filter source families and the Ovid-to-PubMed translation warning.
assertions: do not paste ovid syntax into pubmed
do not silently modify these filters

rules.rule: prisma-s-reporting.md owns the 16-item PRISMA-S mapping, including multi-database (Item 2), full strategy (Item 8), and peer review (Item 14).
tiab-expansion.md owns objective term-ranking interpretation (coverage/lift/background) and PubMed proximity-operator behaviour.
audit-template.md defines the numbered PubMed line-set section; audit_markdown.py renders it from concept_blocks (tested in test_audit_markdown.py::LineSetAndAppendixTests).

audit-template.md defines the paste-ready PRISMA-S appendix block; audit_markdown.py renders it always-on and can emit it standalone via --emit-appendix (tested in test_audit_markdown.py::LineSetAndAppendixTests).
examples.md owns the worked PICO/PECO/PCC concept-gate walk-throughs.

The full intake policy for pasted Boolean syntax / line sets / field-tagged queries lives only in SKILL.md 'Required Input'. workflow.md and goal-tracking.md link to it instead of restating it. A bare contextual mention of 'Boolean syntax' is allowed anywhere; the duplicated *restatement* phrasing is not. Distinct facets (concept-evidence in concept-analysis-and-gating.md; framework-choice in framework-selection.md) are separate rules and stay.
assertions: never use pasted boolean terms, operators, filters, line numbers, field tags, or line structure

signatures: ignore the syntax and ask the user to restate
ignore the syntax and ask the user to restate

rules.rule: The six-field stage-banner definition is canonical in SKILL.md. workflow.md's numbered stage map is the single source for the references in force at each stage; inline stage triggers say 'references per the stage map' instead of re-listing them. Collapsed in Phase 3.
workflow.md owns the build sequence. mesh-and-pubmed-tools.md keeps only a tool-to-stage map that links to workflow.md, not a restated sequence. Collapsed in Phase 1.
assertions: formal concept analysis and concept gate
